response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled web and full-stack engineer with genuine strengths in distributed systems and agile delivery, but the role's core requirement — deep, production-grade C++ development — is nearly absent from his background. His only C++ exposure is a brief contract at National Instruments (7 months) and a student thesis project involving wave-field synthesis. There is no evidence of STL mastery, multi-threaded systems design, or message-broker-based C++ applications. Equally disqualifying is the complete absence of industrial automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 security standards, MQTT, and the industrial protocols listed as key competencies. His expertise sits firmly in the TypeScript/Node.js/web ecosystem, which does not transfer to this role's systems-level requirements.
strengths: Proven experience designing and operating high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems at Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, millions of HTTP requests per second).
Solid event-driven architecture experience through multiple roles and the GISCollective open-source platform.
Strong software-craftsmanship discipline: TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, and a demonstrated record of mentoring others.
concerns: C++ experience is marginal and dated — limited to a 7-month contract at National Instruments and a thesis-era side project; no production C++/STL systems work is evident.
No exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL practices, IoT protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, DNP 3.0, IEC 61850), or embedded/on-premises platform development.
Core tech stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS) is largely orthogonal to the systems-programming and industrial-cybersecurity competencies the role demands.

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation or IoT domain experience
